summary: Great‑sounding front‑of‑house recording of an auspicious early‑1979 show, with a solid performance overall despite a criticized Bob Weir slide.
review: The FOB mix is praised as clear and well balanced, capturing the upbeat mood of what reviewers call an auspicious start to a new era for the Dead. The concert itself is described as very good, though one reviewer notes that Bob Weir’s slide work was sub‑par. Overall the show is enjoyable and the recording quality enhances the listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed 4‑star review highlights a strong performance and excellent FOB sound, with only minor criticism of a single instrumental moment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house (FOB) mix praised as great sounding, clear and well‑balanced; no technical flaws reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewer notes a very good overall show with high energy despite rainy conditions, indicating a smooth and engaging set flow.

band_member_comments.Bob: His slide guitar was described as 'godawful', the only negative comment in an otherwise positive review.
